<<THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE CONSTANTS A, B, AND C IS DETERMINED BY THE FACT THAT THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY OCCURS AT T = 3 SECONDS. THIS MEANS THAT THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF V(T) EQUALS ZERO AT T = 3. THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF V(T) IS 3AT² - 2BT + CT + D. SETTING THIS EQUAL TO ZERO GIVES 3A(3)² - 2B(3) + C(3) + D = 0. THIS RESULTS IN 27A - 6B + 3C + D = 0. THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN A, B, AND C IS 27A - 6B + 3C + D = 0.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED FROM T = 0 TO T = 5 SECONDS IS CALCULATED BY INTGRATING THE VELOCITY FUNCTION FROM 0 TO 5 AND DIVIDING BY 5. THE INTEGRAL OF V(T) FROM 0 TO 5 IS THE INTGAL OF AT³ - BT² + CT + D FROM 0 TO 5. THIS EQUALS (A/4)(5⁴) - (B/3)(5³) + (C/2)(5²) + D(5). DIVIDING THIS BY 5 GIVES THE AVERAGE SPEED. THE AVERAGE SPEED IS [(A/4)(625) - (B/3)(125) + (C/2)(25) + 5D]/5. THIS CAN BE SIMPLIFIED TO (A/16) - (B/60) + (C/10) + D. THE AVERAGE SPEED IS (A/16) - (B/60) + (C/10) + D.>>

<<THE LETTER W APPEARS FOUR TIMES IN THE ABOVE RESPONSE. THE WORDS ARE: "THE", "AVERAGE", "SPEED", "THE".>>

IS THERE ANYTHING ELSE I CAN HELP WITH?
